name: Bleeding Tooth Fungus
primary_sources: scientific descriptions by Howard James Banker
Walter Henry Snell and Esther Amelia Dick

visual_attributes.morphology: funnel-shaped cap with a short, thick stem
distinctive_features: bright red guttation droplets on young fruit bodies
teeth-like projections on the underside of the cap
hairy, velvety texture on young caps
visual_attributes.texture: velvety when young, smooth when mature
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: cap width 4 to 20 cm, height up to 10.5 cm
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: cap atop a short stem, with spines hanging from the cap's underside

domain_specific_functional.ectomycorrhizal_association: exchanges soil nutrients for carbon from host trees

relationships.description: Forms mutualistic relationships with coniferous trees, exchanging nutrients.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: known for its unusual appearance and inedibility
primary_sources: scientific descriptions by Howard James Banker

contrastive_constraints: NOT a smooth-capped fungus without spines
